daily.baseball_pulse:
**Most Actionable Fantasy Intel:**

**Injury Watch & Roster Moves:** Kyle Stowers (Orioles) suffered a hamstring injury during spring training—monitor his status closely as we near Opening Day. Kyle Harrison (Giants) is dealing with a blister issue; timeline unclear but worth tracking. Carlos Estévez (Royals) continues to show velocity concerns, sitting just over 90 MPH on his fastball—5 MPH below last season's average with a week to go. This is a red flag for a high-leverage closer in early drafts. Alek Manoah is working through a fingernail issue. Multiple depth pieces (Blake Perkins, River Ryan, Jasson Dominguez, Yerry De los Santos, Kenedy Corona, Payton Henry) have been optioned/reassigned to minor leagues, clearing roster spots.

**Opening Day Starters Locked In:** Chris Sale (Braves), Tanner Bibee (Guardians), Joe Ryan (Twins), Cristopher Sanchez (Phillies), Logan Webb (Giants), and Yoshinobu Yamamoto (Dodgers) are confirmed for Opening Day. Yamamoto looked sharp early in spring action. Salvatore Perez is back and active for the Royals after returning to the lineup.

**Spring Training Standouts & Stream Candidates:** Jake Bauers (Brewers) is having an exceptional spring with multiple power displays—monitor if he gets consistent AB's. Salvador Perez homered in his return. Tyler Soderstrom (Athletics) is back in Cactus League action after injury. Cole Young (Mariners) has 5 spring HRs. Lawrence Butler is returning to the outfield. These are low-rostered names worth checking waiver wire depth charts.

**Bottom Line:** Most actionable items are injury-related (Stowers, Estévez, Harrison watch) and opening day pitcher confirmations. The feed is heavy on prospect highlights and spring training noise with limited roster-impact intel—standard pre-season churn. Focus on monitoring Estévez velocity and Stowers status before Week 1.
